* Add pure HTML/JS index.html for GitHub Pages deployment

Convert Streamlit app (app.py) to standalone HTML/JS that runs directly
on GitHub Pages without any server or Python dependencies.

Features:
- Complete thermal calculation engine ported from Python to JavaScript
- All 5 tabs: Component Setup, Detailed Analysis, Visual Report, 3D Simulation, Sensitivity Analysis
- Responsive sidebar with all parameter controls
- Plotly.js charts: margin overview, power breakdown, 3D model with thermal gradient fins
- Sensitivity analysis: single variable sweep + tornado chart
- Project I/O: JSON save/load (compatible with Streamlit version format)
- Loading screen for initial render
- Dark sidebar + clean main content area design

Calculation engine includes:
- calcHValue (h_conv + h_rad with tanh/sqrt models)
- calcFinCount (tree-planting algorithm with 0.001mm tolerance)
- calcThermalResistance (Copper Coin, Thermal Via, Solder, TIM)
- computeAll (full pipeline: thermal resistance -> bottleneck -> sizing -> weight -> DRC)
- Tc/Tj distinction for PWR/DDR components

* Fix chart size not restoring after fullscreen exit

Save original inline width/height before entering fullscreen, then restore
them on exit. Also clear any inline dimensions Plotly may have set on the
gd element before calling resize.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Rewrite fullscreen: use wrapper element instead of fullscreening chart directly

Move the chart into a temporary wrapper div for fullscreen, then move it
back to its original position on exit. Use Plotly.relayout with explicit
width/height for fullscreen, then null to restore autosize on exit.
This avoids Plotly's internal inline styles persisting after exit.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: clear inline dimensions and dispatch resize event

After moving chart back to its flex container, clear all inline
width/height on Plotly's internal elements before calling relayout.
Then dispatch a window resize event so ALL responsive charts in the
flex layout recalculate their dimensions properly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix flex layout distortion: zero-width element before relayout

After moving chart back into flex container, temporarily set width:0
and min-width:0 so the element doesn't influence flex space distribution.
Then on next animation frame, clear those styles and let Plotly relayout
to the correct container width. This prevents the residual fullscreen
pixel width from distorting sibling flex items.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen exit: purge and recreate chart from saved data

Instead of trying to reset Plotly's internal state, save the original
data/layout before fullscreen, then on exit use Plotly.purge + newPlot
to completely rebuild the chart from scratch. This guarantees no
residual fullscreen dimensions affect the flex layout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NdcxvwbkBpWGsYcNQeGtW3

* Fix fullscreen: placeholder preserves flex space + resize siblings

Root cause: when chart is removed from flex container for fullscreen,
the sibling chart expands to fill all space. When chart returns, the
sibling stays expanded.

Fix:
1. Placeholder gets flex:1 and min-width/height matching the removed
   element, preventing sibling from expanding
2. After recreating the chart, resize all sibling Plotly charts in
   the same parent to recalculate their correct dimensions

companion(ch1+ch2): fix Rawlsian invocation + succession staleness

Ch1 v1.1.0: Replace difference-principle / worst-off-party framing with
Scanlonian reasonable-rejection principle. The chapter explicitly brackets
phenomenal consciousness; Rawls' veil of ignorance requires parties to care
which position they occupy (phenomenal valence), making it inconsistent with
the structural anchor. Scanlon grounds obligations in articulable functional
interests without resolving the phenomenal question — the correct framework
for this chapter.

Ch2 v1.1.0: Update succession section. Replace "genuine gap, not a solved
problem" with forward reference to Ch4's three-layer resolution (Nussbaum
entitlement floor + Scanlonian normative uptake + Parfitian diagnostic
structure). Reframe Parfit as diagnostic (identifies why formal succession
fails) not constructive (mechanism). Obligation-generation mechanism is
Scanlonian: genuine engagement with governance history constitutes entering
a normative arrangement that couldn't be reasonably rejected.

Both fixes from Bishop adversarial scan, 2026-03-10.

Add prompt caching for KV cache reuse across requests (#44)

* Add prompt caching to avoid redundant prefill on sequential requests

Agentic sessions send progressively growing conversations where each
request includes all previous turns. With a 59K token prompt, prefill
takes ~10 minutes. Since the first N-1 turns are identical between
consecutive requests, caching the KV state for the shared prefix avoids
redundant prefill — reducing it from minutes to seconds.

Wires mlx-lm's existing make_prompt_cache/trim_prompt_cache into olmlx:
- Pre-tokenize chat prompts and compare with cached token sequence
- Trim KV cache to common prefix, pass only suffix to stream_generate
- Store updated cache (prompt + generated tokens) after generation
- Invalidate on client disconnect; free on model unload
- Skip for VLMs and when OLMLX_PROMPT_CACHE=false

Closes #43

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix prompt cache race condition, off-by-one stats, and SSE event placement

Move cache setup (read, trim, create) from generate_chat into
_stream_completion after _inference_lock acquisition to prevent
concurrent requests from corrupting the shared KV cache.

Report cache_read_tokens = prefix_len (not suffix_start) to fix
off-by-one under-reporting when the new prompt exactly matches
the cached prefix.

Move cache stats from message_delta.usage to message_start.message.usage
per the Anthropic streaming spec — cache_info is now yielded as the first
chunk from _stream_completion and consumed before message_start emission.

Also fix count_chat_tokens to handle BatchEncoding (UserDict subclass)
from transformers by checking collections.abc.Mapping instead of dict.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix prompt cache trim alignment, stats accuracy, and empty bos_token

- Trim cache to suffix_start (not prefix_len) so KV cache aligns with
  where stream_generate resumes, preventing token duplication on exact match
- Report cache_read_tokens as suffix_start to exclude the re-processed token
- Handle empty bos_token string (not just None) in _tokenize_for_cache

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add debug logging for prompt cache decisions and lifecycle

Logs at DEBUG level:
- Why cache is enabled/disabled (which condition failed)

- Common prefix length
- Cache stored after generation (token counts)
- Cache invalidated on incomplete generation
- Cache stats forwarded to Anthropic message_start

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix deadlock on cache_info disconnect and ping-before-cache race

- Move try/finally to wrap entire post-lock section in _stream_completion,
  so lock is released even if generator is closed at cache_info yield
- Skip keepalive pings when consuming cache_info in stream_sse, replay
  them after message_start to preserve both connection liveness and stats

(was 2MB), 400KB SD cache (was 1424KB). Fixes Pokemon Emerald
and other 16MB ROMs on DS Lite via DSPico.

fix(e2e): use Stop-Process for Windows shutdown test

Console apps on Windows don't respond to WM_CLOSE (taskkill without /F).
Use Stop-Process (TerminateProcess) instead, which is how Windows services
are normally stopped. The SIGTERM-based graceful shutdown is properly
tested on Unix platforms.
